strengths: Nearly two decades of full-stack experience, far exceeding the 6+ year requirement, with sustained recent work in the core stack: TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB across Optilyz, RIDE, and re:cinq
Hands-on AI agent development in his current role — building MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — directly matching the JD's required AI agent experience

Deep testing and engineering-craft credentials: practiced TDD/CI/CD and trunk-based development professionally, authored open-source testing libraries and a test runner, and taught testing courses — plus cloud (AWS CDK) and Kubernetes/Docker deployment experience
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed, though extensive Node.js backend work suggests a short ramp-up
Kotlin is not on the resume; however, Java appears in both skills and prior robotics work at Altom, covering the JVM secondary-stack need
Vitest/Jest/Playwright are not named, but he built test frameworks from scratch and taught Selenium, indicating testing-tool fluency transfers easily

key_factors.factor: Current AI agent and MCP tooling development

response.justification: The candidate is an exceptionally strong fit for this Senior Fullstack role: he brings roughly 19 years of engineering experience with the last decade centered on the exact primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS), and his current work building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks satisfies the rarest requirement — AI agent development. His polyglot background (TypeScript, Java, C/C++, D, PHP, Swift) matches the multi-language requirement, and his demonstrated craftsmanship — TDD, CI/CD, authored testing libraries, workshops and conference talks — aligns directly with the JD's emphasis on code quality, reviews, and process improvement. The only gaps (Nest.js, Kotlin, specific test runners) are framework-level details well within reach of someone who has repeatedly mastered new stacks and even built his own frameworks.
